mappings.rationaleAI: Crustaceans are mandibulate arthropods forming the subphylum Crustacea (F.14.a), including decapods, copepods, barnacles, krill, and isopods, with Pancrustacea encompassing hexapods.

Encompasses the classification of all living things across viruses (A), monerans (B), protists (C), fungi (D), plants (E), and animals (F), with the three-domain classification of Archaea, Bacteria, and Eukaryota.
Animals (kingdom Animalia) encompass all animal classifications (F), from Porifera (F.2) through Cnidaria (F.4), Arthropods (F.14), Mollusks (F.15), Echinoderms (F.22), to Chordates (F.25).
Seeds are the reproductive units of spermatophytes, connecting to conifers as gymnosperms (E.6) and angiosperms or flowering plants (E.10), with differences in seed enclosure between the two groups.
Biology as the study of life spans all classification groups from viruses (A) through bacteria (B), protists (C), fungi (D), plants (E), and animals (F).
Mollusca encompasses gastropods (F.15.g), bivalves (F.15.e), cephalopods (F.15.h), scaphopods (F.15.f), and placophorans (F.15.d) across the mollusk classification.
Flowers are the reproductive structures of angiosperms (E.10), organized into magnoliopsids/dicots (E.10.a) and liliopsids/monocots (E.10.b).
Plants (kingdom Plantae) cover the full plant classification (E) from bryophytes (E.1) through ferns (E.5), conifers (E.6), and angiosperms (E.10).
Eukaryotes span protists (C), fungi (D), plants (E), and animals (F), all characterized by membrane-bound nuclei and organelles.
Fungi are a kingdom of osmotrophic eukaryotes (D) including yeasts, molds, and mushrooms, characterized by chitin cell walls and extracellular digestion.
Fruits are seed-bearing structures of angiosperms formed from the ovary post-fertilization, directly relevant to flowering plants (E.10).
Algae are photosynthetic organisms classified among protists (C.1), ranging from unicellular microalgae to multicellular macroalgae.
Organisms span all classified living entities from viruses (A) through bacteria (B), protists (C), fungi (D), plants (E), and animals (F).
Zoology covers the structure, classification, physiology, and evolution of all animals (F), spanning invertebrate and vertebrate classifications.
Insects (class Insecta) are hexapod invertebrates classified as uniramians within arthropods (F.14.b), constituting the largest and most diverse animal group.
Birds are warm-blooded theropod dinosaurs classified among chordates (F.25), the sole surviving dinosaur lineage.
Botany studies plants covering the full plant classification (E), from bryophytes through angiosperms, with taxonomy and nomenclature.
Arthropods are the most species-rich animal phylum (F.14), subdivided into crustaceans (F.14.a), uniramians (F.14.b), and chelicerates (F.14.c).
